question: A company tracks average website sign-ups per year. Based on the chart, which of the following statements is true?

options.A: The latest year has the lowest sign-ups in the entire period.
options.B: From this chart, it is not possible to make a reliable conclusion about how the latest full year compares with earlier years.
options.C: The latest year shows a sharp decline compared to previous years.
options.D: The latest year proves that sign-ups are now permanently declining.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 4 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (278 sign-ups) directly to full-year values like 741 sign-ups creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
